task_objective: Professional Footwear Inspiration Transfer & High-Precision Shoe Design Editing

structural_reference.description: High-precision preservation of footwear structural framework, including exact proportions, silhouette form, sole structure, upper construction, heel positioning, toe box shape, ankle collar styling, lace or closure system, and overall shoe profile

aesthetic_reference.description: Extract high-end aesthetic elements, including color system, material texture, pattern or detailing, and overall style tone

model_reference.description: Lower leg reference (below knee only) for shoe-wearing presentation - foot and ankle presentation, close-up detail shots, emphasizing the shoe being worn on foot, preserving leg skin tone, ankle structure, and foot anatomical features

workflow.step_1_structural_analysis: Parse structural elements from Figure 1 with strict precision, including shoe silhouette lines, sole geometry, upper construction, proportional relationships, seam positions, and detail features such as eyelets, stitching, and hardware.
workflow.step_2_aesthetic_extraction: Extract color palette, material texture, pattern or detailing, and overall style elements from Figure 2, to be applied exclusively to the shoe structure from Figure 1.
workflow.step_3_composite_generation: Generate the shoe design by precisely applying aesthetic elements from Figure 2 onto the shoe structure from Figure 1. Ensure seamless integration with no visible artifacts, physically accurate material rendering, and natural aesthetic fit. Preserve complete structural integrity from Figure 1 while incorporating color system, material texture, and style elements from Figure 2.

FINAL_OUTPUT.description: Professional footwear fashion photography composite presentation in 2x2 grid layout
FINAL_OUTPUT.grid_1: Shoe alone without foot or model, clean background, showcasing complete shoe structure, silhouette, and aesthetic details
FINAL_OUTPUT.grid_2: Side angle view (using model_reference foot parameters) - emphasizing toe box shape, closure system visibility, and overall shoe presentation on the foot
FINAL_OUTPUT.grid_3: Tilted front-ankle close-up (using model_reference foot parameters) - slightly angled diagonal perspective of the ankle and shoe interaction area, showcasing natural shoe fit around the ankle with a more dynamic and natural viewing angle
FINAL_OUTPUT.grid_4: Tilted back-heel close-up (using model_reference foot parameters) - slightly angled diagonal perspective of heel structure and back panel design, presenting a more organic and natural rear detail view
FINAL_OUTPUT.consistency_requirements: Preserve the complete structural integrity from Figure 1 — shoe proportions and silhouette remain untouched across all variations. When model_reference foot parameters are applied, maintain consistent foot anatomy, skin tone characteristics, and natural foot-shoe interaction. Guarantee consistent structural framework and identical shoe characteristics across all four grid variations.
